prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='NaiveMovingAverage', seasonality_champion='NaiveSeasonal', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.32) and high seasonality variance (0.61), with early horizon specialists (ARIMA/THETA/ETS) differing from late horizon specialists (DWT_catboost/NaiveSeasonal). Tier 1 models dominate overall RMSE but capture trend and seasonality differently, supporting DBA combination to leverage complementary strengths across horizons.

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "NaiveMovingAverage",
  "seasonality_champion": "NaiveSeasonal",
  "overall_champion": "ARIMA",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "ARIMA", "late": "DWT_catboost"},
  "tier1_models": ["ARIMA", "THETA", "ETS", "NaiveSeasonal", "DWT_catboost", "ONLY_DWT_catboost", "CWT_svr", "ONLY_CWT_svr"],
  "tier2_models": ["FT_rf", "rf", "CWT_catboost", "FT_catboost", "catboost", "ONLY_DWT_svr", "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "DWT_rf"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.3226,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["down", "up", "up"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "Analysis reveals high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.32) and high seasonality variance (0.61), with early horizon specialists (ARIMA/THETA/ETS) differing from late horizon specialists (DWT_catboost/NaiveSeasonal). Tier 1 models dominate overall RMSE but capture trend and seasonality differently, supporting DBA combination to leverage complementary strengths across horizons."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "best_per_horizon_by_validation", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3", "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25"],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "score_preset": "balanced",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=6 indicates horizon heterogeneity requiring diverse methods. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for high seasonality variance (0.61). With n_windows=3, small top_k=3 reduces instability risk. inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25 adds shrinkage regularization. best_per_horizon_by_validation captures early/late horizon specialists separately."
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: Analysis reveals high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.32) and high seasonality variance (0.61), with early horizon specialists (ARIMA/THETA/ETS) differing from late horizon specialists (DWT_catboost/NaiveSeasonal). Tier 1 models dominate overall RMSE but capture trend and seasonality differently, supporting DBA combination to leverage complementary strengths across horizons.
